title: Investigate OpenAI/Codex MCP parameter compatibility vs Claude
description:
Investigate why MCP tool parameter handling appears to misbehave on the OpenAI/Codex path while Claude/Anthropic historically did not.

Known symptoms from this session:
- create_task with persistent intended as false ended up creating .mxd/tasks/*.json persistent definitions because "false" appears to have been treated as a truthy string
- update_task calls behaved as if empty-string optional params (old_description/new_description/parentId) were treated as provided, causing mutual exclusion / reparent validation errors
- The user reports Claude did not historically have these issues

Goal:
Determine whether the root cause is in provider-side tool calling differences, our Zod->JSON schema conversion, tool-call argument serialization/deserialization, or MCP/tool wrapper behavior.

Scope:
- Compare Anthropic vs OpenAI/Responses tool definition formatting and returned tool args
- Inspect actual code paths from tool schema generation to handler invocation
- Add focused tests that reproduce the bad behavior if possible
- If a clear fix is straightforward, implement it; otherwise produce a precise diagnosis with the exact failing layer

Why:
If OpenAI/Codex path corrupts optional/boolean tool params, orchestration becomes painful and tool semantics differ by provider, which is unacceptable.
